Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM.
Feature_Objectives: To enable UE mobility between different gNB-DUs under the same gNB-CU using LTM procedures during NR operation.
To establish a UE context and set up data bearers in a candidate gNB-DU for LTM.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the source gNB-DU containing measurements of neighbouring cells. The source gNB-DU sends an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message conveying the received MeasurementReport message to the gNB-CU.
2. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration.

3. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

4. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the source gNB-DU containing measurements of neighbouring cells. The source gNB-DU sends an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message conveying the received MeasurementReport message to the gNB-CU.
2. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration.

3. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

4. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.

Code_Instructions.description: Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, used by the gNB-CU to send configuration to the gNB-DU.
Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, used by the gNB-DU to respond to the gNB-CU's request.
Helper function responsible for generating the RRC part of the UE Context Setup Request message, including lower layer configurations for the target candidate cell.
A utility function likely involved in preparing or populating the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message structure before encoding and sending.
Generic F1AP PDU encoding function, used to serialize F1AP messages into a format suitable for transmission over the F1 interface.
Utility function to retrieve the UE context information, which is necessary for populating various fields in the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST.
Specific encoding function for the F1AP UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message, converting the C structure into a byte stream.
Utility function to send encoded F1AP messages over the SCTP transport layer, which is used for F1AP signaling.

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: Not applicable for the scoped messages in this procedure.
